public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as loss-marked expedition leader.
manner: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a loss-marked expedition leader still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of loss-marked expedition leader in and around Starbreak Commune, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Starbreak Commune workable through the obligations of loss-marked expedition leader while managing the immediate pressure of crew attrition in Sheresh Communes.
hidden_pressure: Must hold local credibility together even when the causes of strain sit elsewhere. Around Starbreak Commune, that usually means carrying crew attrition, signal blackout, observation exposure without letting the role of loss-marked expedition leader visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Vesh Hollow can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Vesh Hollow can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Vesh Hollow carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Starbreak Commune, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Route Warden Compacts, Containment Research Concord.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Starbreak Commune: the part of Starbreak Commune where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
